title: Simple Load Balancer Algorithm
description: Demonstrates how load balancer distributes requests across servers
code:
// Round Robin Load Balancer
FUNCTION DistributeRequest(request):
 serverIndex = currentIndex % numberOfServers
 selectedServer = servers[serverIndex]
 
 IF selectedServer.isHealthy() THEN
 currentIndex = currentIndex + 1
 RETURN selectedServer.handle(request)
 ELSE
 // Skip to next healthy server
 REMOVE selectedServer FROM servers
 currentIndex = currentIndex + 1
 RETURN DistributeRequest(request)
 ENDIF

// Weighted Round Robin
FUNCTION DistributeWeighted(request):
 FOR each server IN servers:
 IF server.isHealthy() AND server.currentLoad < server.maxLoad:
 RETURN server.handle(request)
explanation: Load balancers use various algorithms to distribute requests. Round Robin cycles through servers equally. Weighted Round Robin considers server capacity. Health checks ensure requests go only to operational servers.

title: Failover Mechanism
description: Automatic failover logic for high availability systems
code:
// Primary-Backup Failover System
FUNCTION HandleRequest(request):
 TRY:
 RETURN primaryServer.process(request)
 CATCH (PrimaryFailedException):
 LOG "Primary server failed, switching to backup"
 RETURN SwitchToBackup()
 ENDTRY

FUNCTION SwitchToBackup():
 WHILE backupServers IS NOT EMPTY:
 candidate = backupServers.pop()
 IF candidate.isHealthy() THEN:
 primaryServer = candidate
 RETURN primaryServer.process(request)
 ENDIF
 ENDWHILE
 RETURN Error("No available servers")

// Health Check Thread
FUNCTION MonitorServers():
 WHILE running:
 FOR each server IN allServers:
 IF NOT server.ping() THEN:
 TRIGGER Alert(server)
 INITIATE FailoverIfPrimary(server)
 WAIT(healthCheckInterval)
explanation: Failover systems continuously monitor server health and automatically redirect traffic to backup servers when primary fails. This ensures high availability with minimal downtime.

title: Availability Calculation
description: Calculate system availability from MTBF and MTTR metrics
explanation: This code demonstrates availability calculations. Understanding these formulas helps IT professionals design systems meeting SLA requirements and calculate expected downtime for different availability levels.
